title: BasalCellDemo Vignette
text: Manuscripts written within basalcelldemo_rtools converted HTML using pkgdown published https://basalcelldemo.readthedocs.io/en/latest/r_api/. files somewhat isolated documentation built Sphinx, actively recommend creating vignettes standard .R script documentation (e.g., using roxygen2). (can achieve functionality using .ipynb files, can integrated built seamlessly Sphinx). However, demonstrated file, can still add vignette creating .Rmd file including YAML header tags. Like .Rmd file, can write content using standard Markdown (.md) syntax embedding R code inside code blocks. Please use feature purposes creating tutorials R scripts.

title: BasalCellDemo Vignette
code: sca_ <- function(a, f) {   function(x) a * f(x) }  add_ <- function(fp, fq) {   function(x) fp(x) + fq(x) }  mul_ <- function(fp, fq) {   function(x) fp(x) * fq(x) }  f1 <- function(x) {   x }  f2 <- function(x) {   x^2 }  f3 <- function(x) {   x^3 }  v <- 1234 w <- 5678

title: BasalCellDemo Vignette
code: add_(f1, f2)(v) == add_(f2, f1)(v) #> [1] TRUE add_(f1, f2)(w) == add_(f2, f1)(w) #> [1] TRUE

title: BasalCellDemo Vignette
code: add_(add_(f1, f2), f3)(v) == add_(f1, add_(f2, f3))(v) #> [1] TRUE add_(add_(f1, f2), f3)(w) == add_(f1, add_(f2, f3))(w) #> [1] TRUE

title: BasalCellDemo Vignette
code: fis <- function(x) {   0 } add_(f1, fis)(v) == f1(v) #> [1] TRUE add_(f1, fis)(w) == f1(w) #> [1] TRUE add_(f2, fis)(v) == f2(v) #> [1] TRUE add_(f2, fis)(w) == f2(w) #> [1] TRUE

title: BasalCellDemo Vignette
code: get_inverse <- function(f) {   sca_(-1, f) } add_(f1, get_inverse(f1))(v) == fis(v) #> [1] TRUE add_(f1, get_inverse(f1))(w) == fis(w) #> [1] TRUE add_(f2, get_inverse(f2))(v) == fis(v) #> [1] TRUE add_(f2, get_inverse(f2))(w) == fis(w) #> [1] TRUE

title: BasalCellDemo Vignette
code: mul_(f1, f2)(v) == mul_(f2, f1)(v) #> [1] TRUE mul_(f1, f2)(w) == mul_(f2, f1)(w) #> [1] TRUE

title: BasalCellDemo Vignette
code: mul_(mul_(f1, f2), f3)(v) == mul_(f1, mul_(f2, f3))(v) #> [1] TRUE mul_(mul_(f1, f2), f3)(w) == mul_(f1, mul_(f2, f3))(w) #> [1] TRUE

title: BasalCellDemo Vignette
code: fim <- function(x) {   1 } mul_(f1, fim)(v) == f1(v) #> [1] TRUE mul_(f1, fim)(w) == f1(w) #> [1] TRUE mul_(f2, fim)(v) == f2(v) #> [1] TRUE mul_(f2, fim)(w) == f2(w) #> [1] TRUE

what: Create a sorted and deduplicated named vector — sorted_setnames
title: Create a sorted and deduplicated named vector — sorted_setnames
text: Extracts unique name-value pairs vectors, sorted reference vector. particularly useful generating clean, ordered color maps (named vectors) `ComplexHeatmap` annotations data frame.

title: Create a sorted and deduplicated named vector — sorted_setnames
Create a sorted and deduplicated named vector — sorted_setnames
text: values vector values setNames (e.g., color hex codes). names vector names setNames. sort_by vector used define sorting order output.

title: Create a sorted and deduplicated named vector — sorted_setnames
text: named vector elements `values` names `names`,   sorted `sort_by` deduplicated `names`.

title: Create a sorted and deduplicated named vector — sorted_setnames
code: df <- data.frame(   leiden = c("2", "1", "1", "3", "2"),   color = c("#00FF00", "#FF0000", "#FF0000", "#0000FF", "#00FF00"),   celltype = c("B cell", "T cell", "T cell", "NK cell", "B cell") ) # Create a color map for celltypes, ordered by leiden clusters sorted_setnames(df$color, df$celltype, df$leiden) #>    T cell    B cell   NK cell  #> "#FF0000" "#00FF00" "#0000FF"
